title: Agent runners component catalog
description: Optional components layered on top of the single base runner (ubuntu-claude-code). The base agent is always installed; components are selected per-agent via the AGENT_COMPONENTS env list. Profiles (profiles.json) are named presets of these slugs. The portal vendors this file; agent-runners is the single source of truth.

slug.description: Stable id persisted in agents.components and threaded into AGENT_COMPONENTS. Resolves to base/components/<slug>/.

kind.description: Install dispatch class. runtime = service/binary install via the component's own scripts; packs = knowledge-pack install (needs the SB server); toolchain = OS-level dev tool (apt/repo). MCP plugins are a separate role-driven catalog (plugins.json), not components.

description.description: One-line helper text in the wizard.

requires.description: Other component slugs that must also be selected. The wizard auto-checks these; unchecking a required-by component unchecks its dependents.

unlocks.description: Portal-level abilities this component enables. sidebutton-server unlocks dispatch + capabilities; absence ⇒ capabilities=[] and the agent is not dispatchable.

chip.description: Fleet/detail dep-chip descriptor. live=true chips get a status dot from a process signal; live=false are static 'installed' declarations.

config_files.description: Runtime config files this component consumes from a default target path on the agent (e.g. a WireGuard .conf). The portal surfaces these as drop-zones; the agent auto-applies whatever lands at target_path (boot-time + a systemd path-unit watcher). Absent ⇒ the component needs no operator-supplied config.

config_file.description: A single config-file need declared by a component. The agent is pre-configured to consume files from target_path whenever they exist there; delivery (portal or manual SSH) validates against this declaration.

id.description: Stable id for this config within the component (e.g. 'wg-profile'). Unique per component.

label.description: Human label for the portal drop-zone.

description.description: One-line helper text explaining what the file is.

accept.description: Accepted filename suffixes, incl. the dot (e.g. ['.conf']). Empty/absent ⇒ any.

secret.description: true ⇒ the file carries a secret (private key/credentials); the store encrypts it and never returns it to the client after save.

scope_default.description: Default delivery scope in the portal Files hub. 'workspace' shares one file across the workspace's agents; 'agent' pins it per-agent.

target_path.description: Absolute path the component consumes from. A trailing '/' marks a DIRECTORY that accepts multiple named files; no trailing slash marks a single pinned FILE (e.g. '/etc/sidebutton/rdp.env').

multiple.description: true ⇒ target_path is a directory holding several files (e.g. named tunnels); false ⇒ a single file.
